MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Hughes for Perminder Singh.

Mr. Hughes stated that they have a new buyer for the property, that the sale is in escrow and that he will give attention to the issue of Mr. Sarmiento's preliminary distribution. Status on the Petition for Final Distribution was continued to December 26, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing The Petition For Final Distribution Or The Petition For Settlement Of The Eigth Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar May-16-2011 Off Calendar

Petition For Settlement Of Seventh Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Hughes for Perminder Singh.

Mr. Hughes stated that the estate had not been closed because there was an issue with the undeveloped property, that there was a question of whether the property would pass a perculation test, and that distributiion of 50% of the property was held in the Bernice Davis estate which was escheated to the State. The Court granted the Petition for Settlement of the Seventh Report of Status of Administration (subject to review of the order). Status of filing of the Petition for Final Distribution or the Petition for Settlement of the Eighth Report of Status of Administration was set for May 16, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Settlement Of Seventh Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Oct-25-2010 Continued To Dec-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Hughes for and with Perminder Singh.

The Parties stated that they had a new contract/Buyer, that the sale was contingent on the Buyer paying for the percolation test, that it was a County approved test which required a permit, that there were two estates involved and that they needed more time. Counsel requested that the matter be continued so that it could be heard with the Bernice Davis Estate. The matter was continued to December 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Approval Of Second Interim Account And For Second Preliminary Distribution And For Allow. Of Fees
Off Calendar Aug-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Hughes for and with Perminder Singh.

The Court stated that it had a problem with charging interest on the money the Heirs were entitled to, that the estate was opened a long time ago and that the distribution could have been made. Mr. Hughes stated that there were delays due to a dispute with payments made by prudential, the issue of discretionary gifts and the issue of the $1,500.00 commitment to Patches the cat. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Jackson.
